name: Telegram Bot
description: Send Telegram messages, photos, documents, audio, location and polls from any AI agent via the Telegram Bot API. 14 tools, bot-token auth. Outbound + lightweight read of recent updates (no long-polling).
instructions:
This connector uses the official Telegram Bot API (https://core.telegram.org/bots/api).

**Setup**:
1. In Telegram, open chat with **@BotFather** → `/newbot` → pick a display name and a username ending in `bot`.
2. BotFather replies with an HTTP API token like `123456789:ABCDEFghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz`. **Treat as a secret** — anyone with it can send as your bot.
3. Set `TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN` to the full token.

**Authentication model** (Telegram-specific): the bot token goes in the URL path, NOT in a header. The base URL is `https://api.telegram.org/bot${TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN}` and every endpoint is appended as `/methodName`. The adapter substitutes the token via the `{{TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN}}` env-var template in `baseUrl`.

**chat_id model**: every Telegram chat (user, group, channel) has a numeric `chat_id`. For groups and channels the bot must be ADDED first; for users, the user must have sent a `/start` to the bot at least once (Telegram's privacy rule). Once you have a chat_id (extract it from `getUpdates`), pin it.

**Channels**: for **public channels** you can also pass `@channelusername` (with the `@`) instead of a numeric chat_id. Private channels REQUIRE the numeric ID, which always starts with `-100`.

**Markdown vs HTML**: messages support 3 parse modes — `MarkdownV2` (current, escape special chars), `HTML` (`<b>`, `<i>`, `<a href>`), or no formatting. Telegram's `MarkdownV2` requires escaping `_ * [ ] ( ) ~ ` > # + - = | { } . !` — agents that don't escape will get 400 'can't parse entities'. The HTML mode is easier for agents to compose safely.

**File send model**: media tools (sendPhoto, sendDocument, sendAudio, etc.) accept the media as:
  - **HTTPS URL** (recommended on AnythingMCP): Telegram fetches it server-side. URL must be ≤ 5MB for photos, ≤ 20MB for documents via URL.
  - **file_id**: a string returned from a previous send (caching — reuse the same file_id to re-send the same content without re-uploading).
  - **multipart binary**: NOT supported by this connector in a single tool call. Pre-upload elsewhere and pass the resulting URL or file_id.

**getUpdates vs webhooks**: this connector exposes `telegram_get_updates` (long-polling pull) for one-shot reading of recent inbound messages. For continuous inbound stream (chatbot), use Telegram's webhook (setWebhook) and host the receiver yourself — outside the MCP request/response model.

**Rate limits**: 30 messages/sec to different chats, 1 msg/sec to the same chat, 20 messages/minute to the same group. On 429, honor the `retry_after` in the response.

**Out of scope**: payments, inline mode, callback query answers (these usually require a long-running webhook), passport, game scores, sticker management.

tools.name: telegram_bot_get_me
tools.description: Return the bot's own user info: id, username, first_name, can_join_groups, can_read_all_group_messages, supports_inline_queries. Health check + whoami in one call.

tools.name: telegram_bot_send_message
tools.description: Send a text message to a chat. Required: chat_id + text. Use parse_mode='HTML' for safe formatting (`<b>`, `<i>`, `<code>`, `<a href>`). reply_to_message_id quotes a prior message.

chat_id.description: Numeric chat_id OR '@channelusername' for public channels. For users, they must have started the bot first.

text.description: Message text, max 4096 chars. For longer, split into multiple sends.

parse_mode.description: MarkdownV2, HTML, or omit for no formatting. HTML is safer for agents.

disable_web_page_preview.description: If true, no link preview is shown for URLs in the text.

disable_notification.description: If true, send silently (no push notification sound).

reply_to_message_id.description: Quote a prior message by its message_id.

reply_markup.description: Inline keyboard or reply keyboard markup. See Telegram docs for InlineKeyboardMarkup shape.

tools.name: telegram_bot_send_photo
tools.description: Send a photo (JPG/PNG, ≤5MB via URL) by HTTPS URL or by reusable file_id. Optional caption supports parse_mode.

photo.description: Either a public HTTPS URL OR a file_id from a prior send.

tools.name: telegram_bot_send_document
tools.description: Send a document (any type, ≤20MB via URL) by HTTPS URL or file_id. Use for PDFs, spreadsheets, code, etc.

tools.name: telegram_bot_send_audio
tools.description: Send an audio file (MP3, M4A, ≤20MB via URL). Telegram displays it with a music player + duration + title. For voice notes use sendVoice instead.

tools.name: telegram_bot_send_voice
tools.description: Send a voice note (OGG-Opus encoded). Telegram renders it as a microphone bubble with waveform — the canonical 'voice memo' format. Other audio codecs use sendAudio instead.

voice.description: HTTPS URL of an OGG-Opus file OR a file_id.

tools.name: telegram_bot_send_video
tools.description: Send a video (MP4, H.264, AAC, ≤20MB via URL). Telegram streams it inline.

video.description: HTTPS URL of MP4 or file_id.

tools.name: telegram_bot_send_location
tools.description: Send a location point. Optional `live_period` (60-86400 sec) makes the location auto-update in real time for that duration.

live_period.description: Seconds the location stays live (60-86400). Omit for static.

tools.name: telegram_bot_send_poll
tools.description: Send a poll (regular or quiz). For a quiz, set type='quiz' and correct_option_id (zero-based). Anonymous unless is_anonymous=false. Closes after open_period seconds or manually with stopPoll.

explanation.description: Explanation shown when a quiz answer is wrong (≤200 chars).

tools.name: telegram_bot_edit_message_text
tools.description: Edit the text of a previously-sent message (your bot's own messages only). Use to refresh status messages, fix typos, or update interactive UIs without sending a new bubble.

message_id.description: message_id of the message to edit.

reply_markup.description: New inline keyboard (or omit to keep current).

tools.name: telegram_bot_delete_message
tools.description: Delete a message the bot sent (within ~48 hours for groups, anytime for own DMs). Bots cannot delete arbitrary user messages without group-admin rights.

tools.name: telegram_bot_get_chat
tools.description: Fetch chat info: title, type (private/group/supergroup/channel), photo, description, invite_link, member_count, permissions.

tools.name: telegram_bot_get_updates
tools.description: Long-poll recent inbound messages (one-shot — pulls all updates since the last `offset+1`). For continuous inbound, use webhooks instead. Telegram queues updates for 24 hours if not consumed; consuming with this tool clears them.

offset.description: Return updates with update_id >= this value. Pass last_seen_id+1 to ack prior batch.

timeout.description: Long-poll wait in seconds 0-50 (default 0 = short poll). Don't use long timeouts in a request/response MCP.

allowed_updates.description: Update types to receive, e.g. ['message','callback_query']. Omit for all except chat_member.
